content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Debugging for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to debug faster, ship cleaner code, and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates debugging bottlenecks by enforcing strict architectural boundaries and automated observability. By pairing this scaffold with Claude Code's deep context analysis, you can isolate logic errors in minutes, turning chaotic codebases into high-performance, maintainable assets that satisfy even the most demanding technical clients.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, your client’s deadline is looming, and you’re trapped in a labyrinth of recursive errors. You’ve refactored the function three times, but the bug remains—a silent, elusive parasite draining your billable hours and your confidence. Every minute spent chasing a stack trace is a minute you aren't shipping features.

But what if you could bypass the manual hunt entirely? The Antigravity project template isn't just boilerplate; it’s a hardened framework designed for rapid recovery and automated diagnostic injection. By integrating this with the precision of Claude Code, you shift from 'debugging' to 'automated remediation,' reclaiming your time and delivering industrial-grade stability that keeps clients coming back.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Initialize your project with the Antigravity template to establish strict folder structures and logging middleware.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard scaffold, focusing on modular micro-services and centralized error handling.

steps.title: Inject Observability Hooks
steps.description: Configure the template’s built-in observability layer to capture state transitions before failures occur.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add observability hooks to all API controllers to log request state and context metadata into the local debug-trace file.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map the dependency graph and identify where the state corruption originates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current execution flow and pinpoint the exact module causing the unhandled promise rejection in the Antigravity middleware.

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Apply a hotfix using the template's standardized error handling pattern to ensure the patch is non-breaking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a fix for the identified module, ensuring the solution adheres to the Antigravity standard error-boundary pattern.

steps.title: Verify Through Regression Testing
steps.description: Validate the fix against the project's automated test suite to prevent side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the regression suite and confirm that the fix satisfies all edge cases defined in the project architecture.

pro_tips: Always run Claude Code with the --mode=expert flag to receive architectural suggestions rather than just code snippets.
Use Antigravity’s 'Dry-Run' mode to simulate state changes before applying patches to your production environment.
Map your project documentation inside a custom context file for Claude to reference during complex refactors.
Implement a 'circuit breaker' pattern in your Antigravity config to prevent cascading failures during debugging sessions.
Keep your debug-trace files in a separate git-ignored directory to maintain repository cleanliness while preserving diagnostic data.
content.next_steps: Once your project is stable, implement the Antigravity CI/CD pipeline to automate your deployment and ensure no future bugs reach production.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding it. With the right template and AI-driven diagnostics, you can cut your development time in half and focus on what matters: delivering value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
